title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXVIII. Jingles.
content:
Tweedle-dum and tweedle-dee
            Resolved to have a battle,
        For tweedle-dum said tweedle-dee
            Had spoiled his nice new rattle.
        Just then flew by a monstrous crow,
            As big as a tar-barrel,
        Which frightened both the heroes so,
            They quite forgot their quarrel.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCCCXXVIII. Jingles." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
